title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Arctic sea ice is a critical component of the Earth's climate system, (0) which plays a key role in regulating global temperatures through its high surface reflectivity. The decline of this ice cover has become one of the most visible indicators of ongoing climate change. (9) Although seasonal variation in the extent of Arctic sea ice is entirely natural, the long-term trend in minimum summer extent has declined markedly since satellite observations began in the late 1970s. (10) Such is the significance of Arctic ice loss that scientists now speak of a feedback loop in which melting exposes darker ocean water, increasing heat absorption and accelerating further melting. This feedback makes the Arctic particularly sensitive to warming, and (11) Provided that global greenhouse gas emissions are not substantially reduced, models project that the Arctic Ocean may be largely ice-free during summer months within decades. Not (12) only does sea ice loss affect global temperatures through the albedo feedback, but it also threatens the viability of ice-dependent species including polar bears, walruses and certain seal populations. (13) Regardless of short-term natural variability, the underlying trend in sea ice loss is entirely consistent with the predictions of climate models over time. Not (14) until Arctic ice loss began to affect shipping routes and access to offshore resources did Arctic governance receive significant attention from national governments beyond the Arctic states themselves. (15) Notwithstanding the considerable uncertainties in projecting precise timelines, the scientific consensus on the general direction and cause of Arctic change is robust. International scientific cooperation in this sensitive region remains essential. (16) Whether Arctic change is approached primarily as an ecological crisis or as a geopolitical challenge, its implications for the planet as a whole are considerable and growing.

example.text: Arctic sea ice is a critical component of the Earth's climate system, (0) which plays a key role in regulating global temperatures through its high surface reflectivity.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Formal preposition or conjunction meaning 'in spite of' or 'despite', introducing a concessive contrast.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
